name: Sakhalin Oblast (Russia)

colors.symbolism: the seas surrounding the islands and the sky
the snowy winters and purity of the northern lands

description: A light-blue field bearing a white silhouette of Sakhalin Island and the Kuril Islands chain stretching across the centre.
history: The flag of Sakhalin Oblast was adopted on 25 June 1995. It was one of the earliest regional flags of the Russian Far East, with the white silhouette of the islands serving as the central identifier of the territory.

features.symbolism: the blue field represents the surrounding seas (the Sea of Okhotsk and the Sea of Japan) and the sky above the islands.

features.role: outline of Sakhalin and the Kuril Islands
features.symbolism: the geographic territory of the oblast: Sakhalin Island and the Kuril archipelago.
